metadata.summary: Utilities library meant as a complement to django-is-core.

functions.name: chamber.models.fields.generate_random_upload_path
documentation:
Pass this function to upload_to argument of FileField to store the file on an unguessable path.
The format of the path is class_name/hash/original_filename.

classes.name: chamber.models.fields.RestrictedFileFieldMixin

methods.name: chamber.models.fields.RestrictedFileFieldMixin.generate_filename
documentation: removes UTF chars from filename

Same as FileField, but you can specify:
    * allowed_content_types - list of allowed content types. Example: ['application/json', 'image/jpeg']
    * max_upload_size - a number indicating the maximum file size allowed for upload in MB.

methods.name: chamber.models.base.SmartQuerySetMixin.fast_distinct
documentation:
Because standard distinct used on the all fields are very slow and works only with PostgreSQL database
this method provides alternative to the standard distinct method.
\:return: qs with unique objects

methods.name: chamber.models.base.SmartQuerySetMixin.change_and_save
documentation:
Changes a given `changed_fields` on each object in the queryset, saves objects
and returns the changed objects in the queryset.

classes.name: chamber.models.base.SmartModelBase
documentation: Smart model meta class that register dispatchers to the post or pre save signals.

methods.name: chamber.models.base.SmartModel._pre_save
documentation:
---
param change: True if model instance was changed, False if was created
param changed_fields: |-
  fields that was changed before \_pre_save was called (changes in the method do not
  affect it)
---

methods.name: chamber.models.base.SmartModel._save
documentation:
Save of SmartModel has the following sequence:
* pre-save methods are called
* pre-save validation is invoked (it can be turned off)
* pre-save signals are called
* model is saved, model changed fields are reset, is_adding is not False and is_changing has True value
* post-save methods are called
* post-save validation is invoked (it is turned off by default)
* post-save signals are invoked

methods.name: chamber.models.base.SmartModel._post_save
documentation:
---
param change: True if model instance was changed, False if was created
param changed_fields: |-
  fields that was changed before \_post_save was called (changes in the method do not
  affect it)
---

methods.name: chamber.models.base.SmartModel.change
documentation:
Changes a given `changed_fields` on this object and returns itself.
\:param changed_fields: fields to change
\:return: self

methods.name: chamber.models.base.SmartModel.change_and_save
documentation:
Changes a given `changed_fields` on this object, saves it and returns itself.
\:param update_only_changed_fields: only changed fields will be updated in the database.
\:param changed_fields: fields to change.
\:return: self

methods.name: chamber.models.base.SmartModel.get_locked_instance
documentation:
Lock object and reload it from database.
\:return: reloaded locked object from database

classes.name: chamber.models.batch_iterator.BatchCachedQuerysetIterator

methods.name: chamber.models.batch_iterator.BatchCachedQuerysetIterator.__init__
documentation:
Init batch cached queryset iterator
\:param queryset: queryset which you should want to iterate. Queryset should be sortable by ID
\:param key: unique key of your iterator which will be used for cursor caching
\:param store_cursor_with_exception: store cursor in cache if exception will be occurred
\:param batch_size: size of one batch
\:param expiration: expiration in datatime, timedelta or integer (number of seconds) format

Batch iterator that stores last iterated object in the cache.
The iterator may be used in different processes (running at a different time).
Iteration will continue from the last complete batch.

Eq
    iterator = BatchCachedQuerysetIterator(
        User.objects.all(),  # iterated queryset
        'users',             # key used to store last object (cursor) in cache
        batch_size=100,      # size of one iteration
        expiration=10        # expiration of cached cursor in seconds
    )
    # 100 users are returned (if there is more than 100 users)
    list(iterator)

    iterator2 = BatchCachedQuerysetIterator(User.objects.all(), 'users', batch_size=100, expiration=10)
    # next 100 users are returned (users will be sorted by ID)
    list(iterator2)

classes.name: chamber.models.dispatchers.BaseDispatcher

methods.name: chamber.models.dispatchers.BaseDispatcher.__call__
documentation:
`instance` ... instance of the SmartModel where the handler is being called
Some dispatchers require additional params to evaluate the handler can be dispatched,
these are hidden in args and kwargs.

Base dispatcher class that can be subclassed to call a handler based on a change in some a SmartModel.
If you subclass, be sure the __call__ method does not change signature.

classes.name: chamber.models.dispatchers.PropertyDispatcher

methods.name: chamber.models.dispatchers.PropertyDispatcher._validate_init_params
documentation:
No validation is done as it would require to pass the whole model to the dispatcher.
If the property is not defined, a clear error is shown at runtime.
Use this class to register a handler to dispatch during save if the given property evaluates to True.

classes.name: chamber.models.dispatchers.CreatedDispatcher
documentation: Calls registered handler if and only if an instance of the model is being created.

classes.name: chamber.models.dispatchers.StateDispatcher
documentation: Use this class to register a handler for transition of a model to a certain state.

functions.name: chamber.patch.field_init
documentation:
Patches a Django Field's `__init__` method for easier usage of optional `kwargs`. It defines a `humanized` attribute
on a field for better display of its value.

functions.name: chamber.patch.remove_accent_errors
documentation:
Implements the 'remove_accent' error handling (for encoding with text encodings only): the unencodable character
is replaced by an character without accent (characters are converted to ASCII).

classes.name: chamber.importers.AbstractCSVImporter

methods.name: chamber.importers.AbstractCSVImporter.__call__
documentation: file is a required parameter as calling the function without CSV file does not make sense

methods.name: chamber.importers.AbstractCSVImporter.out_stream
documentation:
By default, output stream is essentially turned off by supplying dummy StringIO.
Override this property if you want to direct output somewhere, e.g. in Django commands.

methods.name: chamber.importers.AbstractCSVImporter.get_fields_dict
documentation:
Returns a dict of field name and cleaned value pairs to initialize the model.
Beware, it aligns the lists of fields and row values with Nones to allow for adding fields not found in the CSV.
Whitespace around the value of the cell is stripped.

class_variables.name: chamber.importers.AbstractCSVImporter.skip_header
documentation: By default first line of CSV is assumed to contain headers and is skipped

class_variables.name: chamber.importers.AbstractCSVImporter.fields
documentation: Must correspond to columns in the CSV but columns set dynamically in clean methods can be appended.

class_variables.name: chamber.importers.AbstractCSVImporter.csv_path
documentation: Path to the CSV file relative to Django PROJECT_DIR

Abstract CSV importer provides an easy way to implement loading a CSV file into a Django model.
    * To alter or validate field value, define clean_field_name() method in a similar manner as in Django forms.
    * The class implements __call__ method to allow calling concrete importers as regular functions.
    * __call__ accepts custom CSV path to import different CSV files with the same instance of the importer.
    * all class properties can be set dynamically with getters.

class_variables.name: chamber.importers.BulkCSVImporter.delete_existing_objects
documentation: Set to true if you want to delete all records in the model table

class_variables.name: chamber.importers.CSVImporter.query_fields

class_variables.name: chamber.importers.CSVImporter.update_fields

functions.name: chamber.utils.transaction.smart_atomic
documentation:
Decorator and context manager that overrides django atomic decorator and automatically adds create revision.
The _atomic closure is required to achieve save ContextDecorator that nest more inner context decorator.
More here https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45589718/combine-two-context-managers-into-one

classes.name: chamber.utils.transaction.UniquePreCommitCallable

methods.name: chamber.utils.transaction.UniquePreCommitCallable.join
documentation: Joins two unique callable.

methods.name: chamber.utils.transaction.UniquePreCommitCallable._get_unique_id
documentation:
Callable instance hash is generated from class name and the return value of this method.
The method returns None by default, therefore the class init data doesn't have impact.
You should implement this method to distinguish callable according to its
input data (for example Django model instance)
:return:

One time callable class that is used for performing on success operations.
Handler is callable only once, but data of all calls are stored inside list (kwargs_list).
